content.problemStatement:
An array AAA is called good if the following condition is satisfied:


For all pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤∣A∣1 \le i < j \le |A|1≤i<j≤∣A∣, Ai⊕Aj=∣Ai−Aj∣A_i \oplus A_j = |A_i - A_j|Ai​⊕Aj​=∣Ai​−Aj​∣, where ⊕\oplus⊕ represents the Bitwise XOR operator.
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers. Find the maximum size of a good subsequence of AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output the maximum size of a good subsequence of AAA.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : We can choose the subsequence [3,1,1][3, 1, 1][3,1,1]. It can be verified this is good in the following way:

(i,j)=(1,2)(i, j) = (1, 2)(i,j)=(1,2) : Ai⊕Aj=2=∣Ai−Aj∣A_i \oplus A_j = 2 = |A_i - A_j|Ai​⊕Aj​=2=∣Ai​−Aj​∣
(i,j)=(1,3)(i, j) = (1, 3)(i,j)=(1,3) : Ai⊕Aj=2=∣Ai−Aj∣A_i \oplus A_j = 2 = |A_i - A_j|Ai​⊕Aj​=2=∣Ai​−Aj​∣
(i,j)=(2,3)(i, j) = (2, 3)(i,j)=(2,3) : Ai⊕Aj=0=∣Ai−Aj∣A_i \oplus A_j = 0 = |A_i - A_j|Ai​⊕Aj​=0=∣Ai​−Aj​∣
